prompt:
We're getting throttled by a partner API because our webhook dispatcher just blasts requests as fast as the queue drains. I need a small token-bucket rate limiter, package ratelimit at the root of this module (go-token-bucket), stdlib only.

Shape I need:
- type Clock interface { Now() time.Time; After(d time.Duration) <-chan time.Time } — everything time-related goes through this so we can test without sleeping.
- New(capacity int, refill time.Duration, clock Clock) (*Bucket, error) — bucket starts FULL, regenerates one token every `refill`. Reject capacity <= 0, refill <= 0, nil clock with errors.
- (*Bucket).Allow() bool and AllowN(n int) bool — non-blocking take. n <= 0 is trivially true and consumes nothing; n > capacity is always false (never satisfiable) and consumes nothing; a failed AllowN must not eat tokens.
- (*Bucket).Wait(ctx context.Context) error — block until one token is available, then consume it. If a token is already there, return nil without touching the clock's After at all. Otherwise compute how long until the next token accrues and sleep exactly that long via clock.After — one computed sleep, not a poll loop. ctx canceled while waiting => return the ctx error and do NOT consume anything.

Details that have bitten us with the previous copy-pasted limiter, please get these right: partial refill progress must carry over (if 60% of an interval elapsed at the last check, only 40% remains — don't restart the interval on every call); refill never exceeds capacity; a bucket that sat full for an hour doesn't bank extra credit, but the moment you take a token the next one is due one full `refill` later, not sooner and not later. Everything must be safe under concurrent callers — we run this from a bunch of dispatcher goroutines.

Acceptance contract is ratelimit_test.go (already in the repo, hands off). go test -race -timeout 30s ./... must pass.
